summary: Good‑quality soundboard recording of a high‑energy ’85 show with standout “She Belongs To Me”, “Estimated Prophet” and “Satisfaction” despite some rough spots.
review: Fans rave about the electric second set – “She Belongs To Me”, “Estimated Prophet”, “Sugar Mag” and “Satisfaction” are repeatedly highlighted as fireworks, while Phil’s bass solo in Jack Straw gets special praise. The tape is a soundboard source that sounds solid overall but suffers occasional channel fades and a few bad‑eyes moments, giving it a good but not flawless listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance (many standout songs, enthusiastic fan comments) weighted at 70% and decent soundboard recording with minor glitches weighted at 30% yield an overall four‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and balanced, but reviewers note occasional tape cuts, channel fades and a few “bad eyes” moments that affect specific sections.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is mixed – fun start but some rough numbers (e.g., Revolution, Eyes). The second set shines with tight jams and crowd‑pleasing moments; overall energy builds to a memorable encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals slip on “Revolution” (“butchers the words”), but his guitar work on “She Belongs To Me” is praised as one of his best.
band_member_comments.Phil: Tight, funky playing on “Estimated Prophet”, leads the band in a standout bass solo during “Jack Straw”.
band_member_comments.Bob: "Atomic Bobby" antics highlighted – skipping across stage, wild crowd interaction during “Satisfaction”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s limited appearance (only a few bars of “Baby What You Want Me To Do”) noted; otherwise keyboards not a focus.
band_member_comments.Drums: Interplay between the two drummers noted as “tight” on the jam after “Estimated Prophet”.
